title: Cochleanthes aromatica
intro: Cochleanthes aromatica, also known as the 'Sweet Pepper Orchid', is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique, highly fragrant flowers and attractive foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cochleanthes aromatica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to harness its fragrant oil for therapeutic purposes.
taxonomy: Cochleanthes aromatica is a genus of flowering plants in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This genus consists of several species, including Cochleanthes aromatica. The classification of Cochleanthes aromatica places it within the subfamily Cymbidioideae. Further classification includes Cochleanthes aromatica being within the tribe Cymbidiinae. Within the family Orchidaceae, Cochleanthes aromatica is a member of the clade of orchids that have a characteristic growth habit.
